title: Foundational Theorems of Boolean Algebra
description: A visual guide to the fundamental postulates and theorems that form the basis of Boolean algebra, showing their logical hierarchy and relationships.
relationships: Postulates are the foundational axioms from which all theorems are derived
Single-Variable Theorems (e.g., A+0=A) are proven directly from the postulates
Multi-Variable Theorems (e.g., Commutative, Associative, Distributive) build upon single-variable theorems
De Morgan's Theorems provide rules for complementing complex expressions
The Duality Principle is a meta-theorem that applies to all other theorems and postulates
